risks: ACR admin user is enabled (adminUserEnabled: true), which is a security anti-pattern; Microsoft recommends disabling admin accounts and using managed identity with AcrPull role for AKS-ACR integration
No persistent data layer (database or storage) is included for the e-commerce microservices (catalog, order, payment), which will need state management
No availability zones configured on the AKS agent pool, reducing resilience to datacenter-level failures
No network isolation resources (VNet, private cluster, network policies, or NSGs) are defined, leaving the cluster and services publicly accessible
No Key Vault resource for managing secrets, connection strings, or certificates needed by the microservices
No managed identity configuration for AKS-to-ACR authentication; the plan relies on ACR admin credentials instead of the recommended AcrPull role assignment
correctionsRecommended: Disable ACR admin user (set adminUserEnabled to false) and configure AKS-ACR integration via managed identity with AcrPull role assignment
Add a database resource (e.g., Azure Cosmos DB, Azure SQL, or Azure Database for PostgreSQL) to support catalog, order, and payment service data persistence
Enable availability zones on the AKS agent pool (add availabilityZones property) for improved reliability
Add an Azure Key Vault resource for secure secrets management and integrate it with AKS via Secrets Store CSI Driver
Consider adding a VNet resource with subnet definitions and configuring AKS with network policies for workload segmentation
Add Microsoft Entra ID integration with RBAC for AKS cluster access control and disable local accounts
